name: La Patate
region: Oceania, Polynesia, French Polynesia, Society Islands, Moorea
description: This vibrant dive site off Moorea features a stunning coral garden teeming with life. Schools of butterflyfish, parrotfish, and triggerfish dart among healthy hard and soft corals. The site's shallow depth and calm conditions make it ideal for beginners. Look for moray eels hiding in crevices and the occasional blacktip reef shark patrolling the area. Clear visibility and warm waters create perfect conditions for underwater photography. The site's name comes from its potato-shaped coral formation. Gentle currents allow for relaxed exploration of this thriving ecosystem.

name: Shark Cave
region: Oceania, Polynesia, French Polynesia, Tuamotus, Rangiroa
description: Shark Cave is a thrilling drift dive along a dramatic wall in Rangiroa. Strong currents attract large schools of gray reef sharks, often seen resting in the cave or patrolling the drop-off. The site features vibrant coral formations and frequent sightings of eagle rays and barracuda. Best for experienced divers due to the current, visibility often exceeds 30 meters. The dive starts shallow near the cave entrance before descending along the wall into the blue.

name: Avatoru Pass
region: Oceania, Polynesia, French Polynesia, Tuamotus, Rangiroa
description: This dive site is a thrilling drift dive through a deep pass in Rangiroa’s atoll. Strong currents attract large pelagics like sharks, dolphins, and schools of barracuda. The steep walls are covered in vibrant corals, while the clear water offers excellent conditions. Divers often spot manta rays and eagle rays gliding through the channel. The adrenaline rush of the current makes it a must for experienced divers.

name: Motu Nuhi-Nuhi
region: Oceania, Polynesia, French Polynesia, Tuamotus, Rangiroa
description: Motu Nuhi-Nuhi sits in the famous Tiputa Pass of Rangiroa, where strong currents attract pelagic action. Divers drift along steep coral-covered walls, encountering gray reef sharks, barracuda, and large schools of jacks. The site's depth and flow make it ideal for advanced divers. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, revealing vibrant soft corals and occasional manta rays. The pass acts as a highway for marine life, offering thrilling encounters in crystal-clear Pacific waters.

name: Opunohu Coral Wall
region: Oceania, Polynesia, French Polynesia, Society Islands, Moorea
description: A stunning coral wall plunging into the Pacific, this site near Moorea dazzles with vibrant soft corals and schools of tropical fish. Strong currents bring pelagics like sharks and rays. The wall starts shallow, perfect for all levels, with overhangs and crevices hiding lobsters and moray eels. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, showcasing the dramatic drop-off. Best dived at slack tide for calm conditions.

name: Aquarium
region: Oceania, Polynesia, French Polynesia, Society Islands, Tetiaroa, Tiaraunu
description: A shallow, vibrant coral garden teeming with tropical fish. The clear Pacific waters offer excellent visibility, perfect for snorkelers and beginner divers. Expect colorful parrotfish, butterflyfish, and occasional reef sharks. Gentle currents and sandy patches between coral formations make navigation easy. The site sits near a pristine atoll, surrounded by untouched marine biodiversity. Ideal for relaxed exploration and underwater photography.

The shallow lagoon offers gentle currents, perfect for relaxed drift dives. Unique topography includes sandy channels cutting through healthy coral formations. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, with warm water year-round. The remote atoll location ensures pristine conditions and abundant marine life. Expect encounters with green sea turtles and colorful parrotfish among the thriving reef ecosystem. Ideal for photographers and novice divers alike.

description: This vibrant dive site off Moorea dazzles with steep coral walls plunging into the deep. Schools of eagle rays glide past while blacktip reef sharks patrol the drop-off. The current-swept slope teems with colorful anthias and butterflyfish. Healthy hard corals cover the shallows, where green sea turtles often rest. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters in these clear Polynesian waters. The dramatic underwater topography creates thrilling drift dives along the reef's edge. Night dives reveal octopus and lobsters emerging from crevices. Warm water and minimal surge make this accessible year-round.
